steps.title: Fastest path: try the choices
steps.content: Because the choices give possible solutions, plug them into the equation instead of solving from scratch. The only choice that satisfies the equation is D, $-5+3\sqrt{5}$.

steps.title: Solve the quadratic
steps.content: This is a quadratic equation, so the goal is to find its roots. The quadratic formula is a direct way to solve it when simple factoring is not the focus. Apply the quadratic formula with $a=1, b=10, c=-20$: $$w=\frac{-10\pm\sqrt{100+80}}{2}=\frac{-10\pm\sqrt{180}}{2}.$$ Since $\sqrt{180}=6\sqrt{5}$, $w=-5\pm 3\sqrt{5}$.

steps.title: Identify the matching solution
steps.content: The two solutions are $-5+3\sqrt{5}$ and $-5-3\sqrt{5}$. Of these, only $-5+3\sqrt{5}$ appears among the choices — that matches D — D is correct.
